message:
fix(surface): fix last_cell_width always set to 1 for double-width chars

ClusteredLine::from_cell_iter() was hardcoding last_cell_width to
NonZeroU8::new(1) regardless of actual cell width. For lines ending
with double-width characters (emoji, CJK), this caused incorrect width
tracking that could affect cursor positioning and line wrapping.

Fix: use cell.width() instead of hardcoded 1. Updated test snapshot
to expect last_cell_width=2 for a line ending with 🤢 (double-width).

Also: cellcluster.rs first_cell_idx unwrap → unwrap_or(0) for defensive
handling of empty bidi run ranges.

Validated: 306 surface tests pass (was 305+1 failure).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
